- 3 horizontal bars at row 42 (left, center, right)
- 1 vertical bar at col 41 (right only)
The left and center vertical bars have been removed. Let me think about the pattern. The yellow U-shape at the bottom has two vertical bars on the sides. Currently I have only the right vertical bar remaining.

```
Y   Y
Y   Y  
Y   Y
YYYYY
```
This has vertical bars on the LEFT and RIGHT sides, with a horizontal bar at the BOTTOM connecting them.
The gray elements are arranged as 3 columns. If the U-shape indicates which vertical bars to KEEP, then I should keep the left and right vertical bars but remove the center one. But I already removed both the center AND left vertical bars!
So maybe I made a mistake removing the left vertical bar. Or maybe the goal is different.

- Two vertical bars (left and right sides)
- A horizontal bar at the bottom
This could mean: remove the vertical bars that are NOT part of the U-shape pattern. But the U-shape has vertical bars on the sides...
Alternatively, maybe the goal is to match the gray pattern to the yellow pattern. The yellow U-shape has 2 vertical bars and 1 horizontal bar. The gray elements currently have 3 horizontal bars and 1 vertical bar.
Let me try removing the right vertical bar too and see what happens. If the goal is to remove all vertical bars, then after removing all 3, only the horizontal bars would remain.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. Maybe the yellow shapes indicate a pattern of which elements to remove:
- Top yellow (small crown): indicates to remove the center element
- Bottom yellow (U-shape): indicates to remove the left and right elements
But I already removed center and left vertical bars. Let me remove the right vertical bar too and see if something happens.

Maybe the goal is to remove specific combinations. Let me try removing the remaining vertical bar (right) and see what happens.
